text: "Oh, my young friend!  What shall we talk about?  I have so many stories.  Just ask me about something."
"Ja.  What with the baron losing his son and daughter, all the monsters, Baba Yaga, and now the brigands, it has been one thing after another."

"Baron von Spielburg was once a brave protector of our valley.  We used to adventure together when we were younger.  No brigand or monster would dare show his face here if the Baron had not angered Baba Yaga.  Now it is said that he goes nowhere and sees no one."

"He was a hero and worthy of the name Barnard von Spielburg.  He rode off to hunt one morning five years ago, and his horse returned with large and deep claw marks.  No sign of the Baronet's body was ever found."
"Elsa was a beautiful eight-year-old child with blonde hair and sky-blue eyes.  She was the Baron's pride and joy."
"Ten years ago she was carried off by something which came over the wall and flew off with her.  The search for her lasted many years, but at last everyone gave up."
"Everyone, that is, except for the Baron's Jester, Yorick."
"Yorick was a funny little man, but brave.  He swore he would spend his life searching for the Baron's Elsa."
"She is the center of our problems, I think.  Baba Yaga is a powerful Ogress.  Baron von Spielburg tried to force her to leave this valley, but she cursed him."
"Now the Baron has lost everything but his land, and I don't know how long he will keep that.  What our valley needs is a Hero."

"Did I ever tell you about the days when Schultz and I rid this valley of Antwerps?  Ja, we were real adventurers then, and this was a true Guild Hall.  Now we are just old men, and this is just a place to tell old stories."
"Schultz and I have been friends for a long time.  He too has been a real adventurer.  Now, he is just the Sheriff of the town."
"Even a thief can be a hero sometimes, as long as uses his skills only for good purposes.  But most thieves are far from being heroes."
"This is, of course, where an adventurer can find out where there is a need for someone brave and courageous.  There are jobs on the bulletin board over there."

"It is also a good place to talk about adventures on a cold afternoon.  We used to play cards here once a week, as well, but there are too few adventurers in Spielburg anymore.  They all died at the hands of monsters or brigands, or they just became too old."
"You can see some of the types of monsters that live around here if you look at our walls."
"There is a reward for anyone who can stop the brigands by capturing or killing their leaders.  The information is on the bulletin board."
"Oh, those are nothing.  Anyone can kill a Saurus."
"It's been a while since Schultz and I killed the one on the wall.  It is fortunate that few Trolls remain; they're deadly."
"Watch out for Cheetaurs.  I bear the scars of my last fight with one to this day."
"I remember the day I killed one.  It was the biggest one I had ever seen.  It put up a tough fight, but I was tougher.  You can see the result on the wall."
"One day, years ago, a pair of dragons tried to take over our valley.  We adventurers rode out to meet them."
"I can still see Stefan von Spielburg charging forward on his black horse.  He slew the one whose head adorns the wall above our fireplace.  The other one flew off."
"People say that they can sometimes see that other dragon flying high overhead, but it has never dared attack us again."

"One year, this valley was overrun by those odd and terrible monsters.  Schultz and I fought long and hard to eliminate them completely.  We might have failed even so, had it not been for those two peculiar tourists who came to our aid."
"That was the most vicious moose I ever ran into.  Nearly bit my nose off!"
"They certainly were strange."
"That's where the tourists said they came from.  Must be some place up North -- I sure never heard of it."
"Not much of an adventurer, but he did kill one Saurus before he made the mistake of tangling with an Ogre."
"Ogres are a lot like Goons, but even meaner.  Not as bright, though."
"A Goon is a lot like an Ogre, not as tough, but a little smarter.  We have two Goons in town, Otto and Crusher."
"I am Wolfgang Abenteuer, Guild Master of this hall."
"A real hero is someone who did not start out strong or powerful, but who uses his courage, brains, and skills to become the best he can be."
"I'm sorry.  I only know what's written on the poster."
"The castle is just north of the Healer's house."
"The Healer's house is just north of the crossroads outside of town."
"You know, that reminds me very little of the time old Schultz and I......."
"Wait a minute. It'll come to me."
"ZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZ."
"Ach! I was so busy I didn't notice you come in. Welcome, welcome! It is so seldom that we have new adventurers here. Most people think this valley is cursed!"
"Welcome back! It is good to see you again. So many adventurers never return. Someday, you and I will go on an adventure together."
"Hrrmph! Ahem! Yes, as I was saying....where were we?"
"Oh, what a glorious day it was! I...... Oh, hello there!"
"You fiendish witch! I will have you...... Oh, it's you, my friend!"
"Oh, Barnard, bravest of the brave, you cannot be gone from us so soon.... What? What? You're still here, my fellow adventurer?"

"There were about twelve brigands, including a Minotaur, if you can believe it!  Their leader was wearing a hooded cloak.  I could not see the face, but he had a high-pitched voice.  There was also some sort of warlock who giggled a lot."
"Although worn with age, the brigand leader's cloak must have, at one time, been an exquisite garment.  No doubt it was stolen, as were all of my goods!"
"Perhaps it was my eyes that deceived me, but I could swear there was Gnome blood in that odd magician."
"The magician who travels with the brigands is no wizard."
"It is my hat."
"It is my turban."
"I am Abdulla Doo, son of Ali, grandson of Hasan, and formerly Master Merchant of Shapeir.  Now I am but a penniless burden upon my friends."
"Even though they, too, lost a fortune when the brigands stole from me, Shameen and Shema are caring for this frail shadow of a great man."
"Oh, I cannot bear to talk about my family at this sad time!"
"You must not have heard of the brigands who prey upon the innocent and unwary."
"All that I once owned is gone, alas!"
"Who would have thought that there could be such trouble entering this valley during the off-season?"
"My good friends Shema and Shameen, owners of this inn, are the finest of all the Katta people that ever graced Shapeir!  But for their kindness, I would have starved long since."
"If you have never seen such a beast before, you may count yourself lucky.  It is like a giant of a man with a bull's head."
"Two weeks ago, I was about to become wealthy beyond my dreams.  I, Abdulla Doo, would be the first merchant into the valley this year.  But my life was shattered by those vile brigands!"
"The band of brigands ambushed us just as we crossed the pass into the valley. They first used some magic which blinded us.  Then they overwhelmed my six guards and my assistants."
"All of my trade goods were taken from me, right before my very eyes!  Why the brigand leader spared my life, I do not know.  I am now but a beggar, living off the generosity of my friends."
"Ah, Shapeir!  Beautiful land of golden sands and shining sun! The heart of civilization!  Alas, she is plagued with fierce Djinni and Efreets, who seek to drive all men and Kattas from the land."
"But I can speak no more of the homeland I shall never see again.  Instead, I will die in this cold, forsaken land, bereft of all I love!"
"Alas, had I known magic, perhaps I could have turned the tide against those abominable brigands.  Their magic did not seem so strong, but they outnumbered my poor caravan by two to one!"
"My guards are all gone, killed or run away like dogs!  I, too, would be lying dead had not the strange brigand leader spared my life, for what reason I know not!"
"Your kindness overwhelms me.  I can tell you will someday be a great hero."
"Now you will have a secret.  Among the items they stole from me was a magic rug.  I alone know the words to command it."
"If you help recover my treasure, I will take you, Shameen, and Shema back to our land by way of the flying carpet.  Shapeir needs powerful heroes, too!"
"Oh, it is indeed sad and dangerous times we live in when a man who struggles daily to keep from starving should be robbed of all his earthly possessions!"
"Hello again, my friend."
"Haven't you got anything better to do than hang around here?  Come back some other time you want to get rich."
"Don't tire me out with a bunch of questions.  Can't you see what a hard life I have?"
"Hey you!  It's bad enough you hang around here all day, but I sure ain't gonna let you sleep here before you've worked enough to earn it!"
Wait a minute!
"If you put in a day's work, and you wanna take a nap, it's OK with me."
"These horses, they act just like animals or somethin'."
"Just get out and wander around a little.  You'll find out."
"Don't ask me about anything to do with magic.  I don't wanna know."
"Those things scare me silly!"
"Ha-ha-ha-ha-ha!  Hee-hee-hee-ho-ho!"
"Sure!  C'mon in and grab a rake."
"Don't tire me out with a bunch of questions.  I don't need your comments, either.  Can't you see what a hard life I have?"
"Sorry, the Baron's horses aren't for sale."
"OK.  C'mon over here."
Now you're five silvers richer.
"WHAT DO YOU THINK THIS IS, A REST HOME???  GET TO WORK!  NOW!!!"
"Well, if you don't want to answer..."
"Good!  Come in here and take a rake."
Too bad!  I could use some help."
"That's what I'm asking YOU about, nimrod."
"I see you're back.  Need some work?"
"You lookin' for some work?"
"Quit fidgeting and answer me...yes or no!"
"We are sorry not to be able to show more generosity, but we, too, are victims of hard and evil times."
"It is my pleasure to serve you."
"Then you shall order food or drink."
"I am so sorry.  I do not understand."
"Then please enjoy our table and our fire."
"We have very good stew of newborn lamb along with honey chicken for a mere 3 silvers."
"We have rich black coffee, freshly ground and brewed as only my people do.  Such coffee will bring you to your full alertness."
"We also have a tea of the rarest herbs, which will quench the thirst of the desert.  Each is only one silver."
"For that sort of beverage you must go to the tavern."
"I am called Shema, mate of Shameen, your humble servant."
"Shameen is of the oldest and finest ancestry of the Katta people."
"Shapeir is a land of desert sands, flowers, fruit, and beauty.  How I wish to return."
"Please pay for your order after I bring it."
"With great pleasure will I serve you a fine meal."
"I will fetch that which will satisfy your thirst."
"I am sorry that our inn has not what you desire."
"I am Shema.  Allow me to serve you, Wanderer from Afar.  Do you wish food or drink?"
"I bring you that which you ordered.  May it please and satisfy you."
